title: mammillaria beneckei subsp. balsasoides
intro: Mammillaria beneckei subsp. balsasoides is a rare, subtropical cactus species native to Mexico, characterized by its small, round stems covered in short, fine spines and vibrant pink flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the traditional Mexican folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory infections. Mammillaria beneckei subsp. balsasoides can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the plant's stems and flowers in hot water or a solvent, allowing the medicinal compounds to be extracted and utilized for their therapeutic benefits.
